source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - West Delta 73 C Pollution Event

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Oil Discharge via Vent Boom
actions_taken: Platform shut in immediately upon discovery of fluid spraying from Vent Boom
BSEE notified on 25 February 2022 at 1000 hours
Clean Gulf Associates mobilized 95-foot skimming vessel
LSH on Vent Scrubber tested successfully after incident
Cox management discussed General Safety Rules with platform personnel
event.summary: On 25 February 2022 at 0530 hours, a pollution event occurred at West Delta 73 C production platform. An estimated 660 gallons (later revised to approximately 111 gallons) of unrecoverable oil was discharged into the Gulf of Mexico through the platform's vent boom. The incident resulted from a combination of a Water Skimmer float device failure (pinhole defect) and failure of the Level Safety High (LSH) device on the Vent Scrubber to operate as designed. Contributing factor was operator fatigue, with OP2 working 21 hours (0600 on 24 Feb to 0300 on 25 Feb) and becoming extremely fatigued before the incident occurred.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon system with liquid carryover

threats.name: Water Skimmer float device failure allowing abnormal oil passage
Excessive liquid level in Vent Scrubber
Operator fatigue reducing situational awareness and decision-making

consequences.name: Oil discharge into Gulf of Mexico via Vent Boom

controls.name: Level Safety High (LSH) device on Vent Scrubber
controls.barrier_role: Automatic shutdown trigger to prevent overfill
controls.lod_basis: Automatic safety instrumentation designed to trip platform shutdown

supporting_text: the LSH should have tripped and automatically shut in the platform preventing pollution out of the Vent Boom
the LSH device did not operate as designed which allowed fluid into the Vent Boom
This safety device was functioning prior to the incident and did trip hours before on a similar event
The device was tested before and after the event successfully

controls.name: Water Skimmer liquid level float device
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical float to regulate liquid level and prevent carryover
controls.lod_basis: Primary equipment design to control liquid flow

supporting_text: the float device in the Water Skimmer had a pinhole that was not allowing it rise properly
This defect was allowing an abnormal amount of oil to pass through to the Flotation Cell and Vent Scrubber
the level in the Water Skimmer (MBM-1050) was abnormally high

controls.name: Fatigue Management Procedure - Work Stoppage Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent fatigued operators from continuing work
controls.lod_basis: Cox General Safety Rules requiring work stoppage when fatigue observed

supporting_text: OP2 did not follow Cox's procedures to call for a 'all stop' before becoming extremely fatigued
A work stoppage may have allowed the platform to remain shut in so that the liquid carryover issues could be investigated the next day

Fatigue Management: It is our intent that offshore personnel work a scheduled 12-hour shift per 24-hour day... If at any time an employee is observed or self-evaluated to be too fatigued for continuing work, a work stoppage shall be utilized

controls.name: Operator monitoring and communication during platform startup
controls.barrier_role: Operational oversight to detect abnormal conditions
controls.lod_basis: Standard operational procedure for multi-operator platform startup

supporting_text: OP1 advised to OP2 that the level in the Water Skimmer (MBM-1050) was abnormally high
Operators kept the platform shut in and went to the Vent Scrubber to verify the level
They also observed a large amount of oil in the Flotation Cell
OP2 made a call to the Lead Operator at the main facility WD 73 A

controls.name: Electronic control system for recording bypassed devices and alarms
controls.barrier_role: Detection and documentation of safety device status changes
controls.lod_basis: Automated system to track safety device operations and bypasses

supporting_text: This platform does not have an electronic control system that automatically records bypassed devices, trips, and alarms
BSEE is not able to conclude that the device was bypassed

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
